China Chemicals Reset - Structural Shift Underway | Indian Company wise trigger map

- After 3 years of losses, China is forcing discipline in chemicals.

- This is not a narrative shift.
- This is a pricing + supply reset.

What’s Actually Changing ?

1 | Structural shift: Volume → Value
→ China pushing value-added chemicals (fluoro, specialty, polymers)
→ Reducing intensity in low-margin bulk dumping
→ Focus shifting to high-value, complex chemicals where pricing power exists

2 | Supply side reforms are active
→ VAT tweaks + stricter environmental approvals
→ New capacity requires higher compliance → higher cost base
→ Objective: stop irrational pricing and stabilize industry profitability

3 | Three years of losses forced discipline
→ Large part of industry operated at breakeven / sub-optimal margins (FY21 to FY24)
→ Now prioritizing profitability over volume growth, leading to price hikes

4 | Capacity expansion now selective
→ No blanket capex cycle like pre-2021
→ But targeted additions continue in high-demand molecules

5 | Oversupply in specific specialty chains
→ DFPA, Pyroxasulfone, DCTF saw aggressive capacity addition
→ DFPA: ~6kt capacity vs ~4kt demand → prices $18 → $15/kg
→ Pyroxasulfone: >10kt capacity vs ~4kt demand → spot ~$40 vs ~$70 earlier
→ 2,3-DCTF: ~40% price correction
→ Result: sharp price corrections despite broader tightening

6 | Middle East conflict = pricing accelerator
→ Disruptions tightening global supply
→ China using it to push price hikes → forming higher base
→ Normalization expected 6 to 12 months

7 | Bulk chemicals turning structurally favorable
→ Supply tightening + consolidation
→ Phenol, acetone, IPA chains at >70% utilization
→ Current spreads ~1.5 to 2x vs cycle lows
→ Leads to improved spreads and earnings visibility for producers

8 | Margins supported by utilization + spreads
→ High operating rates + limited new supply
→ Even post-normalization, spreads expected 20 to 30% above historical averages

9 | Demand recovery + RM divergence
→ Demand improving in aromatics, epoxy, nitration chains
→ Caustic soda, bromine prices rising globally
→ But input costs (methanol, sulphur) still elevated, pressure in fluorochemicals
→ Exception: R32 supported till CY27 (China plant relocation)

10 | End result: Clear divergence within chemicals
→ Bulk chemicals: pricing power + earnings recovery
→ Specialty chemicals: molecule-specific oversupply + margin pressure
→ China moving up chain → opens base chemical opportunity for India

India Company wise Trigger Map

1 | SRF (Mixed)
→ DFPA oversupply (price $18 → $15)
→ Trigger: Specialty pressure
→ R32 support till CY27 → partial cushion

2 | PI Industries (Negative)
→ Pyroxasulfone oversupply (>10kt vs 4kt)
→ Spot ~$40 vs ~$70 earlier
→ Trigger: Margin compression + competition risk

3 | Navin Fluorine (Negative)
→ Specialty prices down ~40%
→ China capacity additions
→ Trigger: Non contract sales hit + margin pressure

4 | GFL (Negative)
→ Fluoropolymers demand weak + RM inflation
→ R22 remains under pressure
→ Trigger: Margin squeeze continues

5 | Deepak Nitrite (Positive)
→ Phenolics >70% utilization
→ Strong spreads (~1.5 to 2x vs lows)
→ Trigger: Earnings tailwind

6 | Aarti Industries (Positive)
→ Nitration chain prices ↑ ~10%
→ China supply tightening
→ Trigger: Margin expansion + market share gain

7 | Atul Ltd (Mixed)
→ China CAC output cuts
→ RM costs (phenol, caustic) elevated
→ Trigger: Export opportunity + margin watch

8 | Jubilant Ingrevia / Laxmi Organics (Positive)
→ CTPR prices $25 → $40/kg
→ China supply disruptions
→ Trigger: Strong spreads + CDMO tailwind

9 | UPL / Coromandel / Rallis (Positive)
→ Agrochem prices rising post China rationalization
→ Supply tightening globally
→ Trigger: Margin recovery

10 | Chemplast Sanmar (Positive)
→ Catalyst shift cost +$90 to 120/ton
→ Structural cost increase in China
→ Trigger: Industry-wide price increase

MARUTI SUZUKI – MANAGEMENT COMMENTARY (RC BHARGAVA) 🚗📊

DEMAND & GROWTH

• CNG demand remains strong

INDUCTION COOKING PUSH – POLICY SHIFT UNDER PM SURYA GHAR ⚡️🍳

POLICY DIRECTION  
• Exploring support for induction cooktops under PM Surya Ghar scheme  
• Aim: Reduce dependence on LPG imports  
• Electric cooking positioned as hedge against fuel price volatility  

KEY DRIVERS  
• West Asia conflict → LPG supply risk  
• Rising subsidy burden on LPG  
• Push for energy security + electrification  

MARKET OPPORTUNITY  
• 320+ Mn LPG connections in India  
• ~100 Mn under PMUY  
• Large potential for conversion to electric cooking  

POLICY SUPPORT (UNDER CONSIDERATION)  
• GST cut on induction cooktops: 18% → ~5%  
• Lower customs duty on components  
• Boost to domestic manufacturing ecosystem  

SOLAR LINKAGE  
• Rooftop solar rollout enabling daytime electric cooking  
• Integration of clean energy with household consumption  

CHALLENGES / RISKS  
• Grid stability during non-solar hours  
• Consumer behavior shift (traditional cooking habits)  
• Upfront cost: appliances + wiring upgrades  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Structural shift: LPG → Electricity for cooking  
• Creates long-term demand for power + appliances  
• Execution depends on infra + consumer adoption  

Impact: Positive (long-term structural theme)

NETWEB TECHNOLOGIES – STRATEGIC SHIFT TO AI INFRASTRUCTURE 🚀📊

PRODUCT LAUNCH  
• Tyrone ParallelStor Velox – unified data platform  
• Designed for AI, HPC & enterprise workloads  

CORE INDUSTRY SHIFT  
• AI moving from compute-bound → data-bound  
• GPUs faster, but data pipelines lagging  
• Result:  
  • Underutilized GPUs  
  • Slower training cycles  
  • Higher infra costs  

WHAT VELOX SOLVES  
• Unified data layer (flash + disk + cloud)  
• Single global namespace  
• High-speed data access for AI workloads  
• Acts as data backbone for AI infra  

STRATEGIC TRANSITION  
• Earlier: Hardware/server-led (commoditizing)  
• Now: Data infrastructure layer (high margin, sticky)  
• Moving up the value chain  

TARGET MARKETS  
• AI / HPC clusters  
• Government & sovereign AI infra  
• BFSI analytics & risk systems  
• Large enterprise deployments  

RISKS  
• Competition: Dell, NetApp, Pure Storage  
• Execution risk in software layer  
• Long enterprise adoption cycles  

BIG PICTURE  
• AI bottleneck shifting: Compute → Data  
• Data pipelines becoming critical infra layer  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Transition from hardware vendor → AI platform player  
• Success depends on execution + enterprise adoption  
• Potential for higher margins & scalable growth  

Impact: Positive (strategic upgrade, execution dependent)

CHEMICAL SECTOR – NEAR-TERM PRICING OUTLOOK 🧪📊

PRICE TREND  
• Expected rebound in chemical prices this week  
• Driven by spike in crude (Brent higher WoW)  

SUPPLY DYNAMICS  
• Chinese refineries back post maintenance → supply returning  
• Export restrictions still in place → limits global availability  
• Iran supplies off-market → tightening supply  

PRODUCT-WISE VIEW  
• Naphtha: prices likely to remain elevated  
• Methanol: firm pricing outlook  
• Urea: sustained higher levels expected  
• Polymers: cooling trend likely  

KEY INSIGHT  
• Crude-led cost push supporting pricing  
• Supply constraints keeping select chemicals elevated  
• Not a broad-based rally → product-specific divergence  

Impact: Mixed (upstream benefit, downstream pressure)

channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

text:
UAE exits OPEC from May 2026 🛢️

Big signal: more flexibility in oil production decisions

What UAE said:
• Will focus on national interest & market stability
• Continue investing across oil, gas & renewables
• Aim to balance supply-demand, not restrict it
👉 What it means for India 🇮🇳
• Positive bias for oil supply → reduces risk of sharp spikes
• India already less vulnerable (oil imports ↓ as % of GDP)
• Lower volatility = better control on inflation & fiscal
• Supports macros: CAD, rupee stability & growth outlook
But key variable remains ⏳
➡️ Geopolitics + supply disruptions

If flows stay stable → oil spikes likely temporary
If disruption worsens → short-term pressure

👉 Net takeaway:
UAE exit = more supply-side flexibility
For India, that’s structurally more cushion than risk 📉

BUSINESS DYNAMICS

• Greenfield plants still ramping up

• Operating leverage to kick in with utilisation  

Key Positives  
• Strong revenue growth vs industry  
• Robust cash generation, FCF strength  
• Clean balance sheet, low leverage  

Key Risks  
• High commodity costs (copper) impacting margins  
• ROCE dilution vs target levels  
• Rising working capital requirements  
• Delay in greenfield profitability  

Key Takeaway  
• Strong

In India, cable demand of approximately ₹35,000 crore is expected solely from data centers over the next five years.

In the total project cost of a data center, the share of cables and wires is approximately 3% to 4%.

For every 1 GW of data center capacity, the cabling cost will be around ₹3,500 crore to ₹4,000 crore.

By FY31, India is expected to add 8–10 GW of new data center capacity. To meet this 8–10 GW capacity, the total cable demand in the Indian market could reach approximately ₹28,000 crore to ₹35,000 crore.

Source : Apar Industries Q3FY26 Earnings report

Adani Wilmar: Edible Oil business recorded a volume growth of 17% YoY in Q4 FY26

At the time when there was a gas ⛽️ shortage
Chemicals Sector in India , Capex tracker , Deepak Nitrite is doing the largest capex in the sector ( FY25-FY28)

UAE exits OPEC from May 2026 🛢️

Big signal: more flexibility in oil production decisions

What UAE said:
• Will focus on national interest & market stability
• Continue investing across oil, gas & renewables
• Aim to balance supply-demand, not restrict it
👉 What it means for India 🇮🇳
• Positive bias for oil supply → reduces risk of sharp spikes
• India already less vulnerable (oil imports ↓ as % of GDP)
• Lower volatility = better control on inflation & fiscal
• Supports macros: CAD, rupee stability & growth outlook
But key variable remains ⏳
➡️ Geopolitics + supply disruptions

If flows stay stable → oil spikes likely temporary
If disruption worsens → short-term pressure

👉 Net takeaway:
UAE exit = more supply-side flexibility
For India, that’s structurally more cushion than risk 📉

text:
**JAIN RESOURCE RECYCLING (JAINREC) – THE "URBAN MINING" COMPOUNDER**

**STRATEGIC PIVOT: FORWARD INTEGRATION IN COPPER**
• **Current Status:** Transitioning from primary recycling (Scrap to Ingots) to high-value-added products.
• **Phased Commissioning (Unit 3):**
  - **Copper Anodes:** Started Feb 2026 (800 MT/month); doubling to 1,600 MT in Q1 FY27.
  - **Copper Cathodes:** Started Mar 2026 (750 MT/month); doubling to 1,500 MT by Q3 FY27.
  - **Downstream:** Wire rods (600 MT) and Busbars (500 MT) expected in Q1 FY27.
• **Margin Impact:** Shift to value-added products is expected to push EBITDA/MT above ₹55,000 (from ~₹46,000 baseline).

**JOINT VENTURES & RAW MATERIAL SECURITY**
• **Jain CY Circular Solutions (Ahmedabad):** JV with US-based C&Y Group to process 72,000 MT of scrap per annum.
  - **Output:** ~25,000 MTPA of processed copper starting June 2026.
  - **Benefit:** Direct access to C&Y’s 11-12 US-based scrap yards, securing high-quality feedstock.
• **Kuwait Expansion:** Acquired 25% stake in a battery recycling plant (2,000 MT/month) to secure Middle East scrap flows, operational by Q3 FY27.

**MULTI-METAL DIVERSIFICATION & "GREENIUM"**
• **Critical Metals:** Tin capacity doubled to 500 MTPA; Antimony extraction (100 MT/month) from lead bullion slated for Q3 FY27.
• **Green Advantage:** "Zero waste, zero burning" philosophy using green fuels allows the company to target premium "Green Metal" procurement contracts from global OEMs like Apple and Siemens.
• **Market Positioning:** One of the few multi-commodity recyclers (Lead, Copper, Aluminum, Tin, Antimony) globally, reducing dependence on any single metal cycle.

**FINANCIAL SNAPSHOT (9M FY26)**
• **Revenue:** ₹6,438 Cr (↑ 38% YoY).
• **EBITDA:** ₹449 Cr (↑ 65% YoY) | Margin: 7.0%.
• **PAT:** ₹281 Cr (↑ 65% YoY).
• **Return Ratios:** ROE at 30.1% | ROCE at 24.9% (Pre-expansion metrics).
• **Valuation:** Trading at ~40-60x P/E, reflecting a significant premium over peers like Gravita and Pondy Oxides due to its aggressive 40-50% growth guidance.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• JRR is effectively an **"L&T of Recycling,"** managing four massive concurrent expansion projects.
• The strategic shift into **Data Center-grade copper products** (Busbars/Anodes) aligns perfectly with the AI infrastructure boom you’ve been tracking.
• The main monitorable is the **Working Capital Cycle** (currently ~82 days); management expects a drawdown to 60-65 days as the new value-added units stabilize.

**Impact: Highly Positive (Execution-led Re-rating)**

Q4 FY26 FINANCIAL SNAPSHOT
• Revenue: ₹16,666 Mn (↑ 29.4% YoY).

• PAT: ₹756 Mn (↑ 70.3% YoY).
• Efficiency: Finance costs reduced to 3.3% of revenue (vs 4.4% YoY).

SEGMENTAL PERFORMANCE
• Engineering (79% Mix): Revenue ↑ 24% YoY; EBITDA margins at ~11.7%.
• Polymer (9% Mix): Revenue ↑ 17% YoY; Volume growth at 20%.
• Infrastructure (12% Mix): Focus on margin improvement over volume.
• Exports: Hit by West Asia geopolitics (down 70% YoY); being offset by domestic 800kV HVDC and 765kV project wins.

CAPACITY & INNOVATION
• Expansion: Engineering capacity growing from 375k MTPA to 450k MTPA by June 2026.
• R&D Moat: World-first "Dual Tower Test Bed" operational; testing towers up to 1200 kV.
• Digital: SAP S4 HANA implementation completed for better operational control.

INDUSTRY TAILWINDS (THE MULTI-DECADE THEME)
• Domestic: ₹7.9 Lakh Cr transmission investment planned in India.
• Global: High demand for electrification, EV infra, and renewable integration.
• Positioning: Top 10 global T&D manufacturer; major beneficiary of "China+1" strategy.

CORE INSIGHT
• Skipper is at a structural inflection point, shifting from a "Tower Manufacturer" to a "High-Value EPC & Innovation" player.
• The record order book and massive ₹9 Trillion NEP pipeline provide multi-year growth visibility.
• While working capital (debtor days) saw a stretch this quarter, the sharp rise in ROE (14.1%) and EBITDA margins suggests strong operating leverage is kicking in.

Impact: Highly Positive

text:
ETERNAL LIMITED – QUICK COMMERCE (BLINKIT) DEEP DIVE

GROWTH TRAJECTORY
• Historical: 104% CAGR (FY23–FY26); growth moderating on larger base.
• Outlook: Management targets ~60%+ CAGR for next 3 years.
• Scale-up: Implies >4x growth from current levels.
• Market: Under-penetrated; focus remains on 15–20 core cities.

COMPETITION & SEASONALITY
• Competition: Short-term discounting pressure vs. long-term market expansion.
• Seasonality: Q4 is seasonally weakest; fewer days vs Q3.
• QoQ Performance: 8% growth; reflects post-festive dip in high-value items.
• Recovery: Q1 FY27 expected to rebound strongly.

PROFITABILITY MILESTONES (EBITDA MARGINS)
• FY23: –17.8%
• FY24: –3.7%
• FY25: –1.3%
• FY26: –0.6%
• Q4 FY26: +0.3% (Break-even achieved).

MARGIN GUIDANCE
• Mature Markets: Delhi NCR nearing 5–6% steady-state margins.
• Long-term Goal: Management confident in 5–6% aggregate margins.
• Philosophy: Structural profitability; growth remains the primary priority.

CORE INSIGHT
• Achieving EBITDA break-even in Q4 FY26 is the most critical de-risking event for the Blinkit thesis.
• The pivot from "cash burn" to "self-sustaining growth" allows management to combat aggressive discounting by new entrants without depleting the ~₹18,000 Cr cash reserve.
• The >4x scale-up target suggests that the "Quick Commerce" model is effectively cannibalizing traditional e-commerce and local kiranas faster than anticipated.

Impact: Highly Positive

ETERNAL (ZOMATO): Q4FY26 CONSOLIDATED GROWTH

• NOV hit ₹26,880 crore, up 54% YoY.
• Revenue surged 186% to ₹17,680 crore.
• Adjusted EBITDA grew 160% to ₹429 crore.
• Cash reserves remain high at ₹17,972 crore.
• Food Delivery EBITDA margin reached 5.5%.
• Blinkit NOV nearly doubled to ₹14,386 crore.
• Quick Commerce revenue skyrocketed 674% YoY.
• Hyperpure B2B business achieved ₹5 crore EBITDA.

Impact: Positive – Dominant growth with sustained profitability.
Google Breaks ground on India AI Hub, Adani and Airtel in partnership.

FOOD DELIVERY SEGMENT

QUICK COMMERCE (BLINKIT)

• Adjusted Revenue: ₹13,232 Cr (↑ 674% YoY).
• Adjusted EBITDA: ₹37 Cr (Turned positive).
• Milestone: First full quarter of EBITDA positivity.

B2B SUPPLIES (HYPERPURE)
• Adjusted Revenue: ₹978 Cr (↑ 37% LFL).
• Adjusted EBITDA: ₹5 Cr (0.5% margin).
• Status: Operational turnaround with positive EBITDA.

GOING-OUT SEGMENT
• GOV (NOV): ₹2,736 Cr (↑ 47% YoY).
• Adjusted Revenue: ₹277 Cr (↑ 21% YoY).
• Adjusted EBITDA: –₹81 Cr (Investments in District app).
• Focus: Aggressive scaling of the new vertical.

CORE INSIGHT
• Quick Commerce GOV has officially surpassed Food Delivery GOV.
• EBITDA positivity in Blinkit is a massive structural de-risking.
• Massive cash pile of ~₹18,000 Cr provides huge M&A optionality.

Impact: Highly Positive

**BHARTI AIRTEL (NXTRA) – GOOGLE AI HUB PROJECT**

**PROJECT OVERVIEW: THE 1GW HUB**
• **Location:** Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.
• **Scale:** Groundbreaking for India’s first gigawatt-scale AI Hub.
• **Investment:** A cornerstone of Google’s $15 Billion "AI-First India" commitment (2026–2030).
• **Significance:** This is not just a data center; it is a specialized AI compute cluster designed for high-density GPU workloads.

**AIRTEL’S CRITICAL ROLE (NXTRA + ADANICONNEX)**
• **Partnership:** Nxtra by Airtel is collaborating with AdaniConneX to co-develop the campus.
• **Responsibility:** Airtel handles the "Data Center Lifecycle"—from construction and power management to operational connectivity.
• **Infrastructure Lock-in:** Solidifies Airtel’s position as the preferred infrastructure partner for global hyperscalers entering the Indian market.

**INFRASTRUCTURE & CONNECTIVITY STACK**
• **Connectivity:** Deployment of ultra-low latency fiber networks and a next-gen cable landing station to handle massive cross-border AI data flows.
• **Fiber Expansion:** Significant expansion of the America–India fiber corridor to support Google’s global cloud mesh.
• **Energy:** Integration of long-term clean energy solutions, aligning with India’s 500 GW non-fossil fuel target.

**STRATEGIC VALUE CHAIN IMPACT**
• **Hyperscale Leadership:** Nxtra moves from being a colocation provider to a "Strategic Infrastructure Partner" for the world's largest tech company.
• **Revenue Mix:** Diversifies Airtel’s revenue away from pure mobility/telecom toward high-margin, long-term B2B digital infrastructure contracts.
• **AI Readiness:** Positions Nxtra at the forefront of the "Cooling Economy" and high-density power management required for AI chips.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• The Visakhapatnam project is a structural "moat-building" exercise for Airtel. By providing the "land, power, and fiber" for Google’s 1GW hub, Airtel secures a multi-decade annuity revenue stream.
• This partnership highlights the "Convergence of Giants"—where Airtel’s connectivity meets Adani’s power/land and Google’s compute demand.

**Impact: Highly Positive**

**INVESTEC – FOOD DELIVERY & QUICK COMMERCE SECTORAL ANALYSIS**

**RATINGS & VALUATIONS**
• **Eternal (Zomato):** **Initiate BUY** | Target Price: **₹375**
• **Swiggy:** **Initiate HOLD** | Target Price: **₹310**
• **Top Pick:** Eternal is identified as the fastest-growing large-cap name in the consumer universe by both Revenue and EBITDA CAGR.

**THE EXECUTION GAP**
• **Food Delivery:** Swiggy trails Eternal by **18–24 months** in operational maturity and profitability metrics.
• **Quick Commerce:** The gap is "materially wider," with Swiggy trailing by **several years** compared to Blinkit’s execution and dark-store density.
• **Defensibility:** Food delivery is viewed as a defensible moat for now, providing the cash flow to fund the "Survival of the Fittest" war in Quick Commerce.

**EMERGING OPPORTUNITIES**
• **Asset-Light Play:** Shift toward "Emerging Platforms" (e.g., Eternal’s *District* app for going-out) is seen as a high-potential, low-capital opportunity.
• **Productivity:** Focus is shifting toward platforms that can successfully integrate non-grocery categories to boost Average Order Value (AOV).

**CORE INSIGHT**
• Investec’s preference for Eternal over Swiggy stems from the former's ability to reach a "profitability inflection point" much earlier. 
• While the market is large enough for both, Eternal’s lead in the high-stakes Quick Commerce segment (Blinkit) provides it with a structural valuation premium.

**Impact: Highly Positive (Eternal) | Neutral (Swiggy)**

India's Transmission Problem

Rough rule: Every 1 GW of RE needs ~0.6 GW of new inter-state transmission to actually evacuate to load centers. India needs about 280 GW of new interstate transmission capacity by 2030 to support 450 GW of solar+wind. 

Reality today:

1. 50 GW of clean energy is stranded across India that is bigger than Pakistan's entire installed grid.

2. Rajasthan alone: 8 GW stuck, with nearly half curtailed during peak solar hours.

3. FY25 transmission build: 8,830 ckm vs 15,253 ckm target, a 42% shortfall, with inter-state additions at their lowest in a decade. 

4. Solar plants commission in 12–24 months. Inter-state transmission takes 3–5 years.

5. FY26 will likely close around 10,000–12,000 ckm  missing even the revised target which was cut down by 37% in mid year, and missing the original target by 50%. Two consecutive years of severe underbuild while installed RE crossed 250 GW.

India isn't power-deficient. It's transmission deficient.

**MACQUARIE GROUP – QUICK COMMERCE SECTORAL ANALYSIS**

**INDUSTRY DYNAMICS & COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**
• **High Intensity:** Segment-wide competition is expected to remain elevated, fueled by rapid geographic expansion and diversifying product categories.
• **Amazon’s Move:** Amazon Now is scaling to **100 cities** in India, backed by a **$300 Mn** investment commitment. This signals a shift from "optional" to "core" for the global e-commerce giant in the Indian market.
• **Price Wars:** Aggressive scaling by deep-pocketed incumbents is likely to trigger renewed customer acquisition battles and heavy discounting.

**OPERATIONAL & ECONOMIC CONCERNS**
• **Dark-Store Fragility:** Rapid expansion of dark-store networks is outpacing immediate demand growth in some micro-markets, leading to underutilization.
• **Unit Economics:** Pressure on "Take Rates" and rising delivery partner incentives are straining the path to sustainable profitability.
• **Efficiency Hurdles:** Delivery cost efficiency remains the primary bottleneck as traffic congestion and labor costs rise across Tier-1 and Tier-2 cities.

**STRATEGIC EXECUTION PRESSURES**
• **Last-Mile Logistics:** The battle is shifting from "who has the product" to "who has the fastest, most reliable delivery fleet."
• **Inventory Risk:** Expanding into higher-margin non-grocery categories (Electronics, Apparel) increases inventory holding risk and returns management complexity.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• While Quick Commerce (QC) has achieved high consumer adoption, the **"Amazon Entry"** at scale shifts the game from a growth race to a capital endurance race.
• Existing players like Zepto, Blinkit, and Swiggy Instamart face a double-edged sword: they must expand to protect territory, but every new dark store temporarily dilutes their margin profile.
• Unit economics will remain under fire until the industry moves toward a higher **Average Order Value (AOV)** by including high-value non-staple goods.

**Impact: Negative (Sectoral Margin Compression)**

**From India to Africa - Anondita Expands Its Playbook** 🌍

Export growth in healthcare is qualification driven, not volume driven.

Anondita’s entry into South Africa’s government procurement system under a multi-year tender (till 2030) marks a structural shift in positioning.

• 🏛️ Entry into regulated, high-compliance market
• 🌍 Participation in national procurement ecosystem
• 📦 Essential product category with steady demand
• ⏳ Multi-year visibility with repeat order potential

This reflects more than export expansion.
It signals ability to meet global standards, clear audits, and operate within institutional supply chains.

In such markets, scale builds gradually but remains durable.

High entry barriers limit competition and improve demand visibility.

The shift is clear:
from transactional exports → structured global supply participation 📈

**ULTRATECH CEMENT – GOLDMAN SACHS ANALYSIS**

**RATING & VALUATION** • **Rating:** Maintain **BUY** • **Target Price:** ₹13,230 (Revised from ₹13,640)  
• **EBITDA CAGR:** ~14% expected over the next 2 years (FY26–FY28).  

**Q4 FY26 PERFORMANCE METRICS** • **EBITDA per Tonne:** ₹1,167 (Strong performance beating estimates).  
• **Volume Growth:** 9% YoY (Total) | India Cement volumes up 18% YoY.  
• **Realizations:** Improved ~3% QoQ, showcasing pricing power in a competitive market.  
• **Cost Efficiency:** Sequential EBITDA/t expansion driven by strong cost control measures.  

**STRATEGIC CONSOLIDATION** • **Brand Integration:** Successful integration of **India Cements** and **Kesoram** assets completed.  
• **Capacity:** Aggressive roadmap to 200+ MTPA solidifies market leadership.  
• **Efficiency:** Focus on green power (43% mix) and alternative fuels to offset inflationary pressures.  

**RISKS & OUTLOOK** • **Energy Headwinds:** Near-term margins face pressure from higher energy and fuel costs (West Asia conflict impact).  
• **Growth Pipeline:** Long-term outlook remains robust, supported by aggressive capacity additions and operational efficiencies.  

**CORE INSIGHT** • Goldman Sachs maintains a bullish stance on UltraTech, viewing it as the primary beneficiary of India's multi-year infrastructure cycle.  
• While the target price saw a minor downward revision due to energy cost sensitivities, the operational "beat" on EBITDA per tonne (₹1,167) reinforces the company’s ability to manage costs better than peers.  
• The completion of key acquisitions (India Cements/Kesoram) positions them to capture a larger share of the southern and western markets.

**Impact: Positive**

**CITY UNION BANK (CUB) – ELARA CAPITAL ANALYSIS**

**RATING & VALUATION** • **Rating:** Maintain **BUY** • **Target Price:** ₹345 (Revised upward from ₹335)  
• **Outlook:** High re-rating potential supported by consistent execution and improving growth visibility.  

**Q4 FY26 PERFORMANCE HIGHLIGHTS** • **Profitability:** PAT beat driven by strong core operating performance.  
• **Loan Growth:** ₹53,379 Cr (↑ 25.7% YoY | ↑ 9.5% QoQ) — showing sharp credit pick-up.  
• **Deposit Growth:** ₹61,712 Cr (↑ 23.3% YoY)  
• **CASA:** Improved to ~29.6% (Strong low-cost deposit mobilization).  

**ASSET QUALITY & PROVISIONS** • **GNPA / NNPA:** 1.9% / 0.7% (Reflects a robust and healthy balance sheet).  
• **SMA Levels:** Special Mention Accounts (SMA) have declined sharply, indicating lower future slippage risk.  
• **Slippages:** Under control with better-than-industry recovery trends.  

**FUTURE GUIDANCE & METRICS** • **ROA:** Guided at 1.6% – 1.7%  
• **ROE:** Guided at 13% – 14%  
• **Growth Visibility:** Operating metrics suggest the bank is entering a high-growth phase while maintaining credit discipline.  

**CORE INSIGHT** • City Union Bank has successfully navigated past asset quality hurdles and is now pivoting back to an aggressive growth stance.  
• The sharp decline in SMA levels and the 25.7% loan growth indicate that the bank's MSME-heavy engine is firing on all cylinders again.  
• With ROA approaching the 1.7% mark, the bank is trading at attractive valuations relative to its historical averages.  

**Impact: Highly Positive**

• Maintain Sell; TP cut to Rs 2,300
• UBS cuts EPS by 11–13%
• Challenging environment persists
• Refrigerant prices near peak
• Agchem demand remains uncertain
• Rising input costs partly offset by crop prices
• Iran conflict complicates chemicals sector

Impact: Negative — earnings, demand visibility weakened

HDFC SECURITIES ON INDUSIND BANK

Maintain Reduce

Target price ₹760 (revised)

Earnings beat driven by lower provisioning despite weak core

Loan book de-growth continues with weak deposit traction

CASA improves but overall franchise remains weak

Growth recovery expected to be slow

RoA improvement likely to be prolonged

Valuation reflects subdued return ratios

Value the stock at ~0.8x Mar-28 ABVPS

HDFC SECURITIES ON SHRIRAM FINANCE

Maintain Add

Target price ₹1,100 (revised)

AUM growth steady at ~15% YoY led by CV and gold loans

Asset quality marginally deteriorates with higher credit costs

Lower opex offsets weaker other income

Capital infusion by MUFG to support future growth

Loan growth and earnings estimates slightly revised down

Value the stock at ~2.1x Mar-28 ABVPS

Jefferies — Hind Zinc
- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹700
- Mar-quarter EBITDA +27% QoQ; +11% vs Jefferies
- Silver correcting, stabilizing ~$70–80/oz
- Zinc prices up ~12% CYTD
- EPS CAGR 34% (FY24–26); +33% expected FY27
- FY27E EV/EBITDA ~8.6x; above long-term average

HSBC — Hind Zinc
- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹730
- 4QFY26 earnings beat: higher volumes, lower costs
- Interim DPS ₹11/share for FY27
- Zinc-lead guidance steady; silver slightly lower
- Higher sulfuric acid/LME may offset benefits
- Downside: prices, mine grades, royalty, related-party risks

Nuvama — Hind Zinc
- Upgrade: Buy — TP ₹700
- Commodity tailwinds boost earnings
- EBITDA beat +5% on prices, volumes, low costs
- Zinc price est: $3.1k/$3.0k FY27/28
- Silver price est: $75/oz FY27/28
- 8%/9% FY27 vols hedged at $3.2k/$59

Goldman Sachs — Oil

- Raised oil-price forecasts
- Brent now $90/Q4 (was $80)
- Due to prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure
- Iran war caused double blockade of chokepoint
- Daily transits through Strait near zero
- Global oil market significantly upended

CITI — RBL BK

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹390

- RoA flat QoQ at 0.55%; missed estimates
- NIM contracted 22bp to 4.41%
- Elevated credit costs at 2.5%
- Yield compression from repricing and mix shifts
- Advances +11% QoQ; unsecured retail subdued
- ENBD equity infusion is a key catalyst
- Credit costs should normalize in 2H

CLSA — RBL BK

- Recommendation: Hold — TP ₹320

- 4QFY26 PBT ~20% below estimate
- Miss from weak PPOP and high credit costs
- NII growth tepid despite strong loan growth
- Loan yields down 50bp; NII +1% QoQ
- Credit costs steady at 2.5%; card stress persists
- Deposits +16% QoQ (adjusted +12% QoQ)
- Loans +11% QoQ

Jefferies — IDFC First BK
- Recommendation: Buy — TP Rs 82
- Mar-quarter profit Rs 3bn; beat estimates
- Lower credit costs and higher fees aided profit
- Fraud write-off reduced headline profit
- Loan growth could stay ~20%
- Mix derisking and funding costs may limit margins
- Trim estimates ~3%; ROA to ~1% by FY28

Nomura — IDFC First BK
- Recommendation: Buy — TP Rs 85
- Resilient quarter despite fraud overhang
- Margins and asset quality surprised positively
- Deposit growth expected to accelerate
- Core PPOP beat from margins and opex control

CLSA — IDFC First BK
- Recommendation: Hold — TP Rs 73
- 4Q PBT Rs 1.9bn; beat estimate
- Core operating profit largely in line
- Credit costs better than expected
- Gross slippages down 70bp to 2.6%
- Deposit growth muted; SA rate cuts impact
- Loans grew 4% QoQ using excess liquidity
- NIM +17bp QoQ to 5.9%
- Management guides FY27 NIM ~5.75%
- Cut FY27/28 estimates on loan & NIM

UBS — IDFC First BK
- Recommendation: Sell — TP Rs 70
- PAT hit by multiple one-offs
- Asset quality improved sequentially
- Loan growth steady; margins +17bp QoQ
- Management guides deposit improvement
- Cut margin & fee assumptions; lower opex
- Trimmed FY27 EPS ~9%; FY28 largely flat
- Trades ~1.1x FY27E P/BV

Bernstein — Axis Bank
- Outperform — TP Rs 1600

- Healthy quarter; strong wholesale-led loan growth
- Asset quality visibly improved, supporting profits
- Margin pressure continued; fee and trading weak
- NIM decline pace moderated
- Lower slippages; contingent provisioning via tax gains
- RoA improved to 1.58%; RoE ~15%
- Capital positioning provides tactical advantage

Kotak Institutional Equities — Axis Bk
- Buy — TP Rs 1600

- Flat earnings; operating profit down 7% YoY
- One-off provisions linked to global tensions
- Revenue muted despite strong loan growth
- NIM unchanged QoQ; weak non-interest income
- Relative re-rating vs peers already significant

UBS — Axis Bk
- Buy — TP Raised to Rs 1620

- Q4 PAT inline; mixed quarter
- One-offs, lower PPOP, lower core credit cost
- Loan growth 18.5% YoY; LDR +40bp QoQ
- Management reiterates NIM ~3.8% through cycle
- Trades ~1.7x FY27E P/BV; favourable risk-reward

Nomura — Axis Bk
- Buy — TP Rs 1560

- 4QFY26: credit-cost beat saved a soft quarter
- PPOP below estimates; PAT in line
- Asset quality strongly improved
- Loan growth robust, led by corporate
- Forecast RoA 1.6–1.7%, RoE 14–15% (FY27–28)
- Earnings CAGR ~23% (FY26–28)
- Valuation attractive: 1.4x FY28P/B

Macquarie — RIL
- Recommendation: Outperform — TP ₹1,570

- Q4 soft print; Jio subscriber adds supportive
- Mobile 524mn; broadband 27mn (AirFiber 13mn)
- ARPU flat; lags Bharti Airtel
- EBITDA margin +60bp sequentially
- Retail revenue uptick to 14% (FMCG demerged)
- JioMart growth boosted revenue; margin down 60bp
- O2C EBITDA declined despite product margin spike
- Upstream gas EBITDA structurally declining (KGD6 past peak)

MS — RIL
- Recommendation: Overweight — TP ₹1,803

- Energy & chemical tightness could reverse underperformance
- Signs of recovery in chemicals, fuel, retail
- Retail growth better, led by quick commerce
- FMCG revenue 2.2x, US$0.8bn in F4Q26
- Oil-to-chemicals margins underperformed peers
- Upstream missed EBITDA due to higher costs
- Balance sheet in line; F26 capex US$15.3bn

Nomura — RIL
- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,680

- Soft 4Q: O2C and E&P missed estimates
- Jio IPO remains key price catalyst
- Consolidated EBITDA down 4% QoQ
- Stock trades rich on FY28 multiples

ELARA CAPITAL ON INDUSIND BANK

Upgrade to Accumulate

Target price ₹940 (revised upward)

Core operating performance weak but PAT supported by lower credit costs

Loan growth stabilizing after earlier decline

NIM compression and funding constraints persist

Asset quality improving with lower slippages

Transition phase ongoing under new management

Rerating dependent on execution and earnings stability

Valued at ~1x FY28E P/BV

CLSA — RIL
- Recommendation: Outperform — TP ₹1,800

- 4Q PAT 2% ahead of estimates
- O2C and retail weaker than forecasts
- Iran war: availability, volatility, insurance, shipping issues
- O2C margin capture very difficult
- Retail EBITDA growth only 3% YoY
- Cut FY27/28 EPS by 2%
- FMCG & media performance strong but undervalued
- New energy capacity commissioning adds confidence
- Retail & O2C improvement are potential triggers

ELARA CAPITAL ON DCB BANK

Downgrade to Accumulate

Target price ₹220 (revised upward)

Earnings beat driven by lower credit costs

Core performance improving with better margins and efficiency

Loan and deposit growth steady

CASA remains low and key monitorable

Asset quality improving with lower slippages

Structural limitations cap RoE expansion

Rerating dependent on consistent delivery

**Jefferies — Paytm
Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,350**

- RBI formally cancelled PPBL payments bank license
- 2024 restrictions: wallet shut, UPI handles moved
- Investment written off; agreements terminated
- Bank board reset; CEO ex‑IDBI executive
- PPBL assets reported to exceed liabilities
- All Paytm services remain operational

**Bernstein — Paytm
Recommendation: Outperform — TP ₹1,500**

- RBI cancelled Paytm’s banking license (effective Apr 24, 2026)
- Cited persistent operational and governance concerns
- Incremental negative given prior regulatory history
- No earnings impact; investment in PPBL already written off
- Potential path: apply for NBFC/PPI, enable wallets/credit

Kotak Institutional Equities — IndusInd BK
Recommendation: Reduce — TP ₹800

- Mixed quarter; lower slippages (2.3%)
- Credit costs elevated ~2%
- RoE weak at 3% due to soft profitability
- RoE recovery needed for re-rating

Bernstein — IndusInd BK
Recommendation: Outperform — TP ₹1,000

- 4Q: early signs of stabilization
- Asset quality and profitability improving
- Slippages and credit costs sharply down
- Deposit growth turned positive; advances contained
- Management retains medium-term guidance

UBS — IndusInd BK

- Recommendation: Sell — TP ₹730
- PAT beat: lower credit costs and opex
- Loan book -0.5% QoQ; LDR down 160bp
- FY27 growth target: system levels
- Exit FY27 ROA target ~1%
- Trades ~1x FY27E PBV; fully valued

Jefferies — IndusInd BK
- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,100

- Mar-quarter performance encouraging; earnings ahead
- Profit ₹5bn; beat estimates
- Lower credit cost and higher treasury gains
- Leadership and Board resets largely complete
- Expect growth uptick and profit improvement
- Upside from better treasury and lower opex
- Valuation: ~1x FY27 adj. PB — attractive

HSBC — IndusInd BK
- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,100

- 4QFY26: gradual course correction evident
- FY27/28 EPS +20%/+10% forecasts
- Gross and net slippages reduced materially
- Write-offs down; credit costs fall significantly
- MFI slippages still elevated; should decline
- Disbursements picking up across key segments
- Rationalising low-yield corporate loans
- Top management hiring largely completed
- Two EDs and two IDs appointed (subject approvals)

HSBC — IndusInd BK

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,100

- 4QFY26: gradual course correction by management
- FY27/28 EPS +20%/+10% forecasts
- Significant reduction in gross and net slippages
- Write-offs decline; credit costs drop materially
- MFI slippages still elevated; should decline
- Disbursements picking up: vehicle, MFI, housing, SME
- Rationalisation of low-yield corporate loans underway
- Top management hiring largely completed
- Two EDs and two IDs appointed (subject approvals)

Kotak Institutional Equities — Shriram Fin

- Recommendation: Add — TP ₹1,100

- 4QFY26: stable performance reported
- Mindful of macro risks to small businesses
- CV operators and borrowers to be monitored
- Company likely to tread carefully near term
- MUFG deal completion strengthens liabilities
- Credit rating upgrade enhances funding profile

Macquarie — Shriram Fin

- Recommendation: Outperform — TP ₹1,220

- Opex control drove PAT beat
- Conservative margin outlook; growth focus
- Credit cost under wraps
- Credit cost remains key monitorable
- Management cautious on FY27 auto growth
- Targets 18% AUM growth
- AUM growth led by PVs and CVs
- MSME segment likely to moderate

MS — Shriram Fin

- Recommendation: Overweight — TP ₹1,250

- Beat led by operating cost outperformance
- 9M credit cost well below historical average
- Anticipated overlays; built higher 4Q credit costs
- Stressed asset formation higher QoQ, lower YoY
- Assess on four-quarter rolling basis
- NII beat 1%; NIM +3bp QoQ to 8.61%
- Adjusted PPOP beat by 5%
- Credit cost 1.9%; beat estimates
- Adjusted PAT beat 20%; beat consensus 4%

CLSA — Shriram Fin

- Recommendation: Outperform — TP ₹1,150

- PBT beat +3%; lower opex, NII marginal miss
- AUM growth +15%; in line with trend
- MUFG capital to accelerate FY27 AUM growth
- NIM steady QoQ after prior improvements
- Key NIM driver: liquidity reduction not complete
- Liquidity 7% of assets (prior 10%)
- Credit costs 1.9%; asset quality in line
- Net slippage 1.8%; higher sequentially, below FY25

Nomura — Shriram Fin

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,200

- Raising caution
- Management aims 18% loan growth
- FY27 range: 15–18%
- 4Q26 net profit: 8% beat
- Beat due to lower opex and credit costs
- Risks: fuel hike, tepid monsoon, MSME disruption
- Focus on new vehicle segment
- Expected 100bp reduction in cost of funds

HSBC — Shriram Fin

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,200

- 4QFY26: earnings beat; strong cost control
- Result: sharp RoA expansion
- Monitor: uncertain macro and weak monsoon
- Weaker monsoon may hurt growth and asset quality
- AUM CAGR cut to 16% (FY26–28) from 18%
- Driver: slower vehicle sales expectations
- Offset by lower operating cost assumptions (FY27–28)

Nomura — M&M Fin

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹400

- 4QFY26: net profit beat +9% (margin expansion)
- Provision overlays in preparation for FY27
- Cost of funds advantage may wane
- Loan CAGR 16–18% over medium term
- FY27 loan growth built at 14% y‑y

Capital 360 ONE on Welspun Corp

WLCO is the leading player in the US HSAW pipe market, commanding an estimated ~30–35% market share, supported by its local manufacturing presence amid ongoing import tariffs. The exit of two key competitors – Stupp and Evraz – has further strengthened its position, enabling WLCO to secure new orders and expand market share.

Recently, WLCO secured its first order from the data centre industry, marking a strategic entry into a new, high-growth segment and significantly expanding its total addressable market (TAM).

Goldman Sachs — Oil

- Gulf exports normalize by end-June
- Previously expected mid-May normalization
- Slower Gulf production recovery assumed
- Economic risks exceed crude base-case
- Upside risks from oil and product prices
- Unusually high refined-product prices persist
- Product shortage risks significant
- Shock scale is unprecedented
- Q2 deficit: 9.6 million barrels/day
- Brent: $100 Q2, $93 Q3, $90 Q4

Jefferies — Lodha

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,215

- FY27 presales guidance: ₹240bn (+17% YoY)
- Guidance 2% above estimates
- Management: 20% 5-year PAT CAGR
- Builds 10x leasing/datacenter scale
- 4Q P&L: in line with expectations
- Operating cash flow surplus: ₹29.6bn
- Net debt reduced by ₹8.0bn

Nomura — Lodha

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,450

- 4Q: Operationally strong; presales +22%
- Collections +18%; OCF +28%
- Embedded pre-sales margins 34% (vs 32%)
- FY27F: 17% presales growth guidance
- 1GW data-center Powershell announced
- Annuity income to grow 10x in six years
- Reported PAT guidance: 20% CAGR FY26–FY31

HSBC — Hindalco

- Recommendation: Buy — TP ₹1,310

- Novelis newsflow to turn positive in CY26
- Positive visibility on Oswego and Bay Minette
- Hindalco underperformed upstream and downstream peers
- Consensus earnings upgrades likely
- Bull case: Higher LME, stronger Novelis, higher multiples
- Bull fair value: ₹1,670 vs base TP ₹1,310

Jefferies — Steel

- China steel exports down 9% YoY (Jan-Mar)
- March exports down 13% YoY
- China production down 6% YoY (Jan-Mar)
- Market balance improving
- Asian conversion spreads recovering; room to expand
- FY27-28 EPS JSTL/TATA 5–28% above Street
- Further upside for EPS upgrades
- Recommendation: JSTL Top Buy; like steel sector

GS — Metals

- Report: Initiate Coverage - Steel: Cyclical to Structural
- Recommendation: Initiate Coverage

- Initiate Buy: JSW Steel — TP ₹1,490
- Initiate Neutral: Tata Steel — TP ₹210
- Initiate Neutral: Jindal Steel — TP ₹1,335
- Initiate Buy: Shyam Metalics — TP ₹1,065
- Initiate Sell: NMDC — TP ₹84

- JSW Steel: Strong capacity growth; downstream, debt advantage
- Shyam Metalics: Diversified metals exposure; low net debt/EBITDA
- Tata Steel: EBITDA improves till FY28; iron ore uncertainty post-FY30
- Jindal Steel: Capacity ramp-up, cost cuts; valuations appear fair
- NMDC: Diversification slow; negative risk-reward, elevated valuation

Kotak Securities — Axis Bank

- Target Price: ₹1,600
- Earlier Target: ₹1,500
- Recommendation: Buy

- Stable core performance; provisions don't derail thesis
- Few one-offs; internal metrics remain comfortable
- Accelerating growth across segments; NIM impacted
- Significant re-rating vs peers; further outperformance harder

Target Price ₹1180

Earlier Target ₹1160

Recommendation Buy

In-line Core; Headwinds Weigh on Growth/Credit Cost

Growth steady; persistent headwinds may pose risk to 18-20% growth target

Hindustan Zinc — Systematix Snapshot

• Rating: **BUY**  
• TP: **Rs 724**; CMP: **Rs 589**; **22%** upside  

• Valuation: SOTP; zinc/lead 9x EV/EBITDA, silver 12x  
• 4QFY26 Revenue: **Rs 126.9bn** (+43.8% YoY)  

• 4QFY26 EBITDA: **Rs 77.1bn**; margin **56.9%**  

• 4QFY26 PAT: **Rs 50bn** (+68% YoY)  

• FY26 Revenue/EBITDA/PAT: **408/220.6/138.3 bn**  
• Zinc CoP ex‑royalty: **USD 903/t** (4Q), FY26 **USD 959/t**  

• Silver price avg: **USD 84/oz**; silver rev **Rs 40.3bn** (4Q)  
• Volumes: mined **315kt** (4Q); zinc metal **227kt** (4Q)  

• Guidance FY27: mined **1,150kt**, refined **1,100kt**, Ag **680t**  

• FY27 capex: **USD 0.5–0.6bn**; FY26 capex **Rs 36bn**  
• Dividend yield guided ~**5.1%** (FY27–28)  

• Key catalysts: silver tailwinds, CoP savings, smelter ramps  

• Key risks: silver price reversal; project execution delays; geopolitics

CIE Automotive India — Mirae Asset / Sharekhan Highlights

• Rating: Positive; Target **Rs 552** (~**21%** upside)  
• CMP referenced: **Rs 457**  

• Valuation: **16.3x/14.8x/13.6x** CY26/27/28 EPS  
• Market cap: **~Rs 17,300 crore**; 52W **526 / 381**  

• Q1CY26 revenue: **Rs 2,612 cr** (+14.9% YoY)  
• Q1CY26 EBITDA: **Rs 401.9 cr**; margin **15.4%**  
• Q1CY26 PAT: **Rs 248.1 cr**; EPS **6.5**  

• India revenue: **Rs 1,663.5 cr**; Europe: **Rs 948.5 cr**  
• Europe aided by ~**17%** forex benefit  

• CY26 capex plan: **Rs 400–500 cr** (Rs 100 cr spent)  

• Management aims to outperform industry CY26  
• Europe restructuring largely complete; margins normalizing  
• Strategic upside: China OEM localisation; Europe/US recovery  

• Key risks: geopolitical cost pressure; export demand slowdown  

• Shareholding: Promoters **65.7%**; FII **4.73%**; DII **21.32%**  
• Financials (CY26E): Revenue **Rs 10,535 cr**; Adj. PAT **Rs 1,088 cr**

Vidya Wires — Ventura Initiating Coverage

**Buy thesis: growth, deleveraging, rerating**

• Rating: **BUY** at CMP ~INR **74**  
• 24‑month TP: **INR 99** (~**35%** upside)  
• FY25–28 revenue CAGR: **~30.5%**  
• FY28E revenue: **~INR 3,303–3,380 cr**  
• FY28E PAT: **~INR 104 cr**  
• RoIC FY28E: **~34%** (high capital efficiency)  
• PEG: **~0.6x** — attractive growth‑valuation mix  
• Capacity doubling to **37,680 MTPA** by FY27  
• Promoter holding **~72.8%**, no pledge  

**Key catalysts / triggers**

• ALCU plant successful commissioning and ramp  
• Strong power & transmission capex recovery  
• Renewable and data‑center demand acceleration  
• Continued margin improvement and EBITDA/ton gains  
• Rapid net‑debt paydown to near **zero** by FY28  

**Primary risks / downside**

• Copper price spikes impair margins materially  
• Supplier concentration (top vendors ~**60%+**)  
• ALCU execution delays or cost overruns  
• High end‑market concentration: Power ~**48%**  
• Historically tight working capital; cash‑flow risk  

Investment view: high growth, rapid deleveraging, asymmetric upside.

text:
Emkay on Specialty Chemicals - China trip reaffirms our views on anti-involution

China is increasingly shifting its focus from bulk chemicals to value-added products, which could have meaningful implications for Indian specialty chemical players.

The potential impact spans companies such as SRF, Navin Fluorine, Gujarat Fluorochemicals GFL, PI Industries, Deepak Nitrite, Aarti Industries, Atul, Jubilant Ingrevia, Laxmi Organic Industries, Chemplast Sanmar, UPL, Coromandel International, and Rallis India.

**PAYTM – RBI ACTION & BROKERAGE VIEW ⚖️📉**

**RATING & TARGET**  
• Rating: Outperform  
• Target Price: ₹1,500  
• CMP: ₹1,147 → ~31% upside  

**KEY EVENT**  
• RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank (PPBL) license (effective Apr 24, 2026)  
• PPBL to be wound up → no further banking operations  

**REGULATORY BACKDROP**  
• Ban on new customers since Mar 2022  
• Restrictions on deposits & wallet top-ups (Jan–Feb 2024)  
• Issues flagged: governance, compliance, depositor interest  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  

**NEGATIVE (SENTIMENTAL)**  
• Strong regulatory language remains a concern  
• Incremental negative from perception standpoint  

**NEUTRAL (FINANCIALS)**  
• PPBL already non-operational for ~1 year  
• Business separation already executed  
• Write-offs taken earlier → no fresh hit  

**POTENTIAL UPSIDE (OPTIONALITY)**  
• Scope to apply for:  
  • NBFC license  
  • PPI license  
• Enables:  
  • Wallet expansion  
  • Credit product scaling  

**FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY**  
• EPS: FY25 (₹-10.35) → FY26E ₹9.01 → FY27E ₹22.83  
• Revenue/share: steady growth trajectory  

**VALUATION SNAPSHOT**  
• P/E: 127x (FY26E) → 50x (FY27E)  
• High valuation but backed by earnings inflection  

**STOCK PERFORMANCE**  
• YTD: -11.7%  
• 1Y: +26.7%  
• Underperformance vs benchmark persists  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• RBI action largely “known negative” → limited incremental financial impact  
• Business now cleaner, with regulatory overhang reducing over time  
• Key re-rating trigger = execution in credit + financial services  

**Impact:** Neutral to slightly positive — sentiment hit, but fundamentals unchanged 📊

**
Initiating Coverage on Fortis Healthcare, with 19% UPSIDE- Transition behind, growth ahead
